title: How Brooke Briggs Built Two Heart-Centered Businesses While Raising Kids
guestBio: Brooke Briggs is an educator and entrepreneur who founded both OC Farm School and Magic Steps Music. She specializes in creating meaningful, nature-based learning experiences that foster child development and strengthen family connections through sensory-rich education and outdoor play.
excerpt: Discover how educator-entrepreneur Brooke Briggs built two thriving businesses centered on children's development while navigating motherhood. Her journey from music classes to farm-based learning offers inspiring insights for purpose-driven women.
metaDescription: Learn how Brooke Briggs balances motherhood with running OC Farm School and Magic Steps Music, creating meaningful work that serves children and community.
keyTakeaways: Passion and purpose can grow side by side when you align your business with your values.
Building community requires invisible labor that often goes unrecognized but creates lasting impact.
Sensory-rich learning and outdoor play are essential for children's development in today's digital world.
You can successfully balance motherhood and business ownership when your work feels meaningful.
Creating connections to nature and rhythm helps children develop authentic relationships with themselves and others.
Family bonding happens naturally when parents and children engage in shared learning experiences.
Purpose-driven businesses that serve community needs can thrive while making a difference.

pullQuotes.text: Brooke's work is rooted in connection — to nature, to rhythm, and to the soul of childhood.

timestamps.label: Magic Steps Music and early childhood development
The origin story of OC Farm School
The importance of sensory learning and outdoor play

content:
What happens when you combine a deep love for children's development with an entrepreneurial spirit and the demands of motherhood? You get Brooke Briggs, a woman who has masterfully woven together her passions to create not one, but two thriving businesses that serve families and community in profound ways.

In this heartwarming episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with Brooke to explore her journey as the founder of both Magic Steps Music and OC Farm School. This conversation isn't just about business success—it's about creating meaningful work that aligns with your deepest values while navigating the beautiful complexity of motherhood.

Finding Your Sweet Spot: Where Passion Meets Purpose

Brooke's story begins with a simple but powerful realization: children thrive when they're connected to nature, to rhythm, and to the authentic essence of childhood. Her work through Magic Steps Music demonstrates how early childhood development flourishes when families come together in joyful, music-filled environments. These aren't just classes—they're spaces where parents and children create lasting bonds while engaging in activities that support crucial developmental milestones.

What makes Brooke's approach so compelling is her understanding that learning doesn't happen in isolation. When families participate together in musical experiences, children absorb not just the rhythms and melodies, but the joy and connection that comes from shared discovery. This philosophy has become the foundation for everything she builds, proving that businesses rooted in authentic connection have the power to transform communities.

Growing Something Beautiful: The Birth of OC Farm School

The transition from music education to farm-based learning might seem like a leap, but for Brooke, it was a natural evolution. OC Farm School represents her commitment to reconnecting children with the earth and providing the kind of sensory-rich learning experiences that today's kids desperately need. In our increasingly digital world, the opportunity to dig in dirt, observe growing plants, and experience the cycles of nature becomes not just valuable, but essential.

Through OC Farm School, Brooke has created something that goes beyond traditional education. She's crafted an environment where children can develop authentic relationships with the natural world while building confidence, curiosity, and wonder. The farm becomes a classroom where learning happens through touch, observation, and genuine engagement with living systems.

This hands-on approach to education addresses a crucial gap in many children's lives. When kids can plant seeds, watch them grow, and understand where their food comes from, they develop a deeper appreciation for the interconnectedness of life. Brooke recognized this need and had the courage to create a solution that serves families in her community while building a sustainable business.

The Invisible Labor of Building Community

One of the most powerful aspects of this conversation centers on something that rarely gets discussed in entrepreneurship circles: the invisible labor required to build and maintain community. Brooke's work extends far beyond the official hours of classes and programs. She's creating spaces where families can connect, where children can thrive, and where community bonds are strengthened.

This kind of work—the emotional labor, the relationship building, the countless small actions that create belonging—often goes unrecognized. Yet it's the foundation upon which meaningful businesses are built. Brooke's willingness to embrace this aspect of her work demonstrates a mature understanding of what it takes to create lasting impact.

For women entrepreneurs, this conversation offers permission to value and price the full scope of what you bring to your work. Community building isn't a nice-to-have addition to your business—it's often the very thing that makes your work irreplaceable and transformational.

Balancing Act: Motherhood Meets Business Ownership

Perhaps the most relatable aspect of Brooke's journey is her honest exploration of balancing motherhood with business ownership. Running two purpose-driven businesses while raising children requires a particular kind of grace and flexibility. Brooke's approach shows that this balance isn't about perfect time management or having it all figured out—it's about creating work that feels so aligned with your values that the challenges become worthwhile.

When your business serves families and children, the line between personal and professional naturally blurs. Brooke has learned to navigate this integration in ways that enhance rather than complicate her role as a mother. Her children get to see her building something meaningful, contributing to community, and following her passions—lessons that extend far beyond any classroom.

This integration also means that Brooke understands her audience deeply. As a mother herself, she knows what families need, what children respond to, and what gaps exist in traditional educational approaches. This insider knowledge becomes a significant advantage in creating programs and experiences that truly serve.

The Ripple Effect of Meaningful Work

What emerges from this conversation is a beautiful picture of how meaningful work creates ripple effects that extend far beyond immediate business metrics. When Brooke creates a space for families to connect through music, she's not just teaching songs—she's strengthening family bonds and creating positive childhood memories. When she provides farm-based learning experiences, she's not just teaching about plants—she's fostering environmental stewardship and sensory development.

This kind of impact-driven approach to business offers a compelling alternative to traditional entrepreneurship models. Instead of asking what market you can capture, Brooke asked what community she could serve. Instead of focusing solely on scalability, she prioritized sustainability and depth of connection.

For women considering their own entrepreneurial journeys, Brooke's story provides both inspiration and practical insight. You don't have to choose between meaningful work and business success. You don't have to sacrifice family time for professional growth. What you do need is the courage to build something that aligns with your deepest values and serves a genuine need in your community.

Brooke Briggs represents the kind of leadership that emerges when women trust their instincts, follow their passions, and commit to serving others. Her journey reminds us that the most fulfilling businesses often grow from the intersection of personal passion and community need, creating work that nourishes both the entrepreneur and everyone she serves.

quiz.title: What's Your Purpose-Driven Leadership Style?
quiz.description: Discover how you naturally combine your passions with impact, just like Brooke Briggs did when building her heart-centered businesses while raising her children.

questions.question: When you imagine your ideal work life, what excites you most?

options.text: Creating spaces where people feel genuinely connected and supported
Building something sustainable that will have lasting positive impact
Pioneering solutions to problems that others haven't tackled yet
Doing work that perfectly aligns with what you believe matters most

questions.question: How do you typically approach big decisions in your life?

options.text: I consider how it will affect the people and communities I care about
I think through the long-term implications and build from there
I trust my gut instinct about what feels right to explore
I ask myself if it aligns with my core values and priorities

questions.question: What frustrates you most about traditional business approaches?

options.text: They often prioritize individual success over community wellbeing
They focus on short-term gains instead of sustainable growth
They stick to what's always been done instead of innovating
They compromise values for profit and conventional metrics

questions.question: When you're feeling most fulfilled as a leader, you're usually:

options.text: Facilitating meaningful connections between people
Steadily building systems that serve others well
Exploring new territory that others haven't ventured into
Living out your purpose in tangible, daily ways

questions.question: How do you prefer to measure success in your work?

options.text: By the strength and depth of relationships and community I've helped create
By the solid foundation and lasting impact I've built over time
By the innovative solutions and new possibilities I've opened up
By how authentically my work reflects my values and serves my purpose

questions.question: What draws you most to entrepreneurship or leadership roles?

options.text: The opportunity to bring people together around something meaningful
The chance to create something substantial and well-built from the ground up
The freedom to pursue ideas and approaches that feel innovative
The ability to design work that honors both your values and your life

results.title: The Community Weaver
results.description: You have a gift for bringing people together and creating spaces where authentic connections flourish. Like Brooke with her music classes, you understand that the most meaningful work happens when you're building community around shared values. You thrive when your leadership creates ripple effects that touch multiple generations. This episode will inspire you with practical ways to scale your community-building gifts while staying true to your heart-centered approach.

results.title: The Steady Builder
results.description: You believe in growing something beautiful over time, laying strong foundations that will serve others for years to come. Your leadership style focuses on sustainable systems and deep roots rather than quick wins. You understand that meaningful impact requires patience and intentional choices. Brooke's journey from music teacher to farm school founder will resonate deeply with your approach to building purpose-driven work that aligns with your values and grows alongside your life.

results.title: The Intuitive Pioneer
results.description: You trust your instincts about what the world needs and aren't afraid to create something entirely new to meet that need. Your leadership is driven by a deep sense of purpose and willingness to forge your own path. Like Brooke recognizing children's need for nature-based learning, you see gaps others miss and feel called to fill them. This episode will validate your pioneering spirit and show you how to balance bold vision with practical family life.

results.title: The Values-First Visionary
results.description: Everything you do flows from your core beliefs about what matters most. You refuse to compromise your values for conventional success and instead create work that feels deeply meaningful. Your leadership inspires others because it comes from an authentic place of service. Brooke's story of aligning business with family values while making a real difference will speak directly to your heart and give you permission to lead in your own authentic way.
